name: Kyoto
localTips: Get an ICOCA card for buses and trains — Kyoto's bus network is confusing but the card taps onto every line and JR train in the city.
Photography is banned in Gion's private geisha alleys — a local ordinance fines tourists who chase or photograph maiko without consent.
Many top sights are free or near-free to walk through; save budget for temple garden entries (¥400–600), which are almost always worth it.
Tour buses fill the famous sites by 10am — many temples open at 6am, and visiting at opening time can mean having Fushimi Inari nearly to yourself.
Cash is still preferred at small temples, machiya cafés and traditional shops — carry ¥1,000 notes and coins even though cards work in most restaurants.

activities.title: Fushimi Inari Shrine
activities.description: Thousands of vermilion torii gates climb Mount Inari in tunnels dense enough to blot out daylight — free and open 24 hours, with the crowds thinning past the second viewpoint.

activities.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove
activities.description: A quarter-mile path through soaring bamboo stalks that creak and sway overhead — arrive before 8am to walk it without the tour groups.

activities.title: Kiyomizu-dera approach streets
activities.description: Sannenzaka and Ninenzaka's stone-paved lanes climb past traditional sweet shops and pottery stores toward the temple's wooden veranda — free to wander even without entering.

activities.title: Philosopher's Path
activities.description: A canal-side stone path shaded by hundreds of cherry trees, named for a philosophy professor's daily walk between Ginkaku-ji and Nanzen-ji temples.

activities.title: Gion district streets
activities.description: Wooden machiya teahouses line Hanamikoji-dori — walk it around dusk for a chance to glimpse a geiko or maiko hurrying between appointments.

activities.title: Kamo River banks
activities.description: Locals space themselves out along the riverbank in evenly spaced pairs and groups — join them for a picnic, or in summer catch the restaurants' riverside kawadoko platforms above the water.

activities.title: Nishiki Market covered arcade
activities.description: 'Kyoto's Kitchen' — a five-block covered arcade of pickle shops, knife smiths and skewer stalls selling tako tamago and yuba, open and dry regardless of weather.

activities.title: Kyoto Imperial Palace Park
activities.description: The former imperial residence's grounds are a free public park of gravel paths and pine groves — the palace buildings themselves are visible on guided walk-throughs.

activities.title: Yasaka Shrine
activities.description: A vermilion shrine gateway linking Gion to Higashiyama, lit by hundreds of paper lanterns donated by local businesses — host of the summer Gion Matsuri festival.

activities.title: Pontocho Alley
activities.description: A narrow lantern-lit lane squeezed between the Kamo River and Kiyamachi's canal, packed with tiny restaurants and bars behind unmarked wooden doors.

activities.title: Higashiyama district streets
activities.description: Kyoto's best-preserved old town — wooden merchant houses, temple lanes and craft shops stretching from Kiyomizu-dera to Chion-in, especially atmospheric at dusk.

activities.title: Tofuku-ji temple grounds
activities.description: One of Kyoto's five great Zen temples — the free outer grounds and approach through a valley of maples are stunning even without paying to cross the Tsutenkyo bridge.

activities.title: Shirakawa Canal
activities.description: Weeping willows trail into a narrow stone-banked canal behind Gion's teahouses — one of Kyoto's quietest, most photogenic corners, a block from the Hanamikoji crowds.

activities.title: Kyoto Station building & Sky Garden
activities.description: A 15-storey glass-and-steel atrium with an escalator climb to a rooftop Sky Garden — free architecture and skyline views regardless of the weather outside.

activities.title: Hidden machiya courtyard cafe
activities.description: Restored wooden townhouses hide tiny courtyard cafes behind sliding doors — % Arabica's original roastery and countless unnamed spots serve coffee under century-old beams.
activities.insiderNote: Look for a small noren curtain and a single potted plant outside an otherwise anonymous wooden facade — that's usually the tell for a machiya cafe.

activities.title: Showa-era kissaten coffee house
activities.description: Time-capsule coffee houses like Yamamoto Kissaten or Soirée — velvet booths, siphon-brewed coffee and jazz records that haven't changed since the 1960s.
activities.insiderNote: Order the 'morning set' before 11am for toast and a boiled egg at the price of the coffee alone — a tradition that started in Nagoya and spread nationwide.

activities.title: Neighbourhood sentō bathhouse
activities.description: Public bathhouses like Funaoka Onsen (a registered cultural property) survive in older neighbourhoods — mosaic-tiled tubs, saunas and a post-bath coffee milk ritual.
activities.insiderNote: Wash and rinse seated at the taps before entering the communal tub — soap never touches the bathwater itself.

activities.title: Tiny Pontocho izakaya
activities.description: Six-seat counters down Pontocho's side alleys serve grilled fish and Kyoto-style oden to a handful of regulars a night — no English menu, no problem, just point.
activities.insiderNote: A seat charge (otoshi) of a few hundred yen is standard and comes with a small starter dish — it's not a scam, just local restaurant custom.

activities.title: Fushimi sake tasting bar
activities.description: Fushimi's soft spring water has fed sake breweries since the Edo period — small standing bars near Gekkeikan's museum pour flights from a dozen local kura.
activities.insiderNote: Ask for the tasting flight rather than a single bottle — most bars will pour three or four small cups so you can compare breweries side by side.

activities.title: Hōnen-in quiet temple
activities.description: A moss-and-maple temple gate just off the Philosopher's Path that most tourists walk straight past — raked sand mounds change design with the seasons, and it's almost always empty.
activities.insiderNote: The main hall is only open for a few weeks each spring and autumn, but the grounds and gate are free and open year-round — go for the silence alone.

activities.title: Tō-ji temple flea market
activities.description: Kobo-san market fills Tō-ji's five-storey pagoda grounds on the 21st of every month — antique kimono, ceramics and street food stalls locals actually shop at.
activities.insiderNote: Arrive at opening for the best antiques before dealers buy them up; by afternoon it's mostly food stalls and souvenirs.

activities.title: Nishiki Tenmangu hidden shrine
activities.description: A small shrine swallowed into Nishiki Market's east end, its torii gate fused into the arcade roof — locals stop to wash at the water fountain before shopping for produce.
activities.insiderNote: The water flowing from the dragon-head fountain is drawn from the same underground source that supplies Kyoto's tofu makers — locals fill bottles here.

activities.title: Traditional tea ceremony experience
activities.description: A kimono-clad host guides you through the choreography of whisking matcha in a tatami tea room — the etiquette of bowing, turning the bowl and savouring a wagashi sweet.

activities.title: Kimono rental in Higashiyama
activities.description: Dressed and hair-styled by professionals, wander Kiyomizu-dera and the Higashiyama lanes in full kimono — photographers will ask to shoot you along the way.

activities.title: Arashiyama rickshaw tour
activities.description: Pulled through the bamboo grove and riverside lanes by an athlete-guide who narrates history and stops for photos at the Togetsukyo Bridge.

activities.title: Fushimi Inari guided night tour
activities.description: A guide leads you up the torii tunnels after dark with a flashlight, when the shrine turns properly eerie and the daytime crowds have vanished entirely.
activities.bookingQuery: Fushimi Inari night walking tour Kyoto

activities.title: Kaiseki cooking class
activities.description: Learn the seasonal, multi-course logic of kaiseki cuisine from a working chef — dashi from scratch, precise knife cuts and plating that mirrors the season.

activities.title: Nijō Castle
activities.description: The shogun's Kyoto residence, famous for its 'nightingale floors' engineered to squeak and warn of intruders — the Ninomaru Palace interior is a rare surviving example of Edo-period design.

activities.title: Sagano Scenic Railway
activities.description: An open-window trolley train trundles along the Hozugawa river gorge from Arashiyama — 25 minutes of forested cliffs, especially spectacular in autumn foliage.

activities.title: Uji matcha tea plantation tour
activities.description: A half-day trip to Japan's most prestigious tea-growing region — walk the terraced plantations, grind your own matcha on a stone mill and visit Byōdō-in's Phoenix Hall.
activities.bookingQuery: Uji matcha tea plantation tour from Kyoto

activities.title: Gion Corner geisha arts show
activities.description: A one-hour sampler of Kyoto's traditional arts — tea ceremony, koto music, kyomai dance and a bunraku puppet excerpt, performed by working maiko and geiko.
activities.bookingQuery: Gion Corner geisha show ticket Kyoto

activities.title: Zen meditation at a working temple
activities.description: Sit zazen with resident monks at temples like Kennin-ji or Shunkō-in — posture and breathing instruction included, some sessions offered in English.
